channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump says some white South Africans are oppressed, could be resettled in the US. They say no thanks
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — Groups representing some of South Africa's white minority responded Saturday to a plan by President Donald Trump to offer them refugee status and resettlement in the United States by saying: thanks, but no thanks.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Leaders from eastern and southern Africa call for a ceasefire and negotiations in Congo
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — Leaders from eastern and southern Africa on Saturday called for an immediate ceasefire in eastern Congo, where rebels are threatening to overthrow the Congolese government, but also urged Congo's president to directly negotiate with them.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump must not repeat his Kim Jong Un mistake with Iran, security expert warns
item.description: President Trump revealed this week he wants to pursue talks with Iran to end its nuclear program, but one expert says it could be a repeat of when Trump looked to take on North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Department announces $7B arms sale to Israel after Congress blocked initial deal
item.description: The Trump administration announced a $7 billion arms sale to Israel, including munitions and missiles, just days after Congress blocked an initial deal.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Former Hamas hostage released 491 days after he was kidnapped and his family was slaughtered
item.description: Eli Sharabi, who was released after spending 491 days in Hamas captivity, will return home without his wife or two daughters, who were killed on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Concerns spike over weak condition of latest 3 hostages released by Hamas
item.description: The frail condition of the latest three Israeli hostages released by Palestinian militant group Hamas on Saturday as part of the fragile three-weeks-old ceasefire agreement has raised concerns.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump says he wants to negotiate about Ukraine. It's not clear if Putin really does
item.description: Nearly three years after President Vladimir Putin invaded Ukraine, his troops are making steady progress on the battlefield. Kyiv is grappling with shortages of men and weapons. And the new U.S. president could soon halt Ukraine's massive supply of military aid.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A family fleeing violence in eastern Congo sees little hope as rebels grab more land
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — None of Zawadi Sifa's seven children have known life without fear and the constant threat of violence.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hamas releases 3 frail-looking Israeli hostages for 183 Palestinian prisoners under Gaza ceasefire
item.description: Hamas-led militants have released three gaunt, frail-looking Israeli hostages and Israel freed 183 Palestinian prisoners in the fifth exchange of a ceasefire agreement that has paused the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hamas names 3 more Israeli hostages to be freed as ceasefire deal stays on track
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Hamas identified three more Israeli hostages it plans to free as part of the fragile ceasefire agreement, a sign the deal was moving forward Friday even as U.S. and Israeli officials continued calls to relocate Gaza’s population after the war.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: We will have relations with North Korea
item.description: President Trump on Friday said the U.S. will have relations with North Korea, touting his personal relationship with Kim Jong Un, who rules the nuclear-armed country with an iron fist and has soldiers fighting on the front lines of Russia’s war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: A look at the hostages taken to Gaza by Hamas, by the numbers
item.description: Israel and Hamas are in the midst of a six-week ceasefire during which Hamas is releasing dozens of the hostages captured in its Oct. 7, 2023, attack in exchange for hundreds of Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Netanyahu: 'No future' in Gaza unless 'Hamas destroyed' amid concern for hostages
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said Hamas must be 'destroyed,' following discussions with President Trump and lawmakers on the Hill, despite concerns that hard rhetoric could jeopardize hostage releases.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Gaza will be given to US by Israel
item.description: President Trump on Friday asserted that Gaza will be given to the U.S. by Israel, expanding on his plan for an American takeover of the war-torn region that he suggests involves the permanent relocation of Palestinians. The controversial proposal stands to upend decades of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Autocratic regimes 'relishing' USAID gutting: Samantha Power
item.description: Samantha Power, a former administrator of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), says autocratic regimes across the world are “relishing” the Trump administration's efforts to gut the agency. “It's not even an opinion.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: International Criminal Court condemns Trump-imposed sanctions
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) on Friday condemned President Trump’s executive order imposing sanctions on the global tribunal. Trump ordered the sanctions in response to the ICC issuing arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Israel's former defense chief.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: One of the victims of Sweden's deadliest mass shooting called his fiancée to say he loved her
item.description: OREBRO, Sweden (AP) — Wounded and bleeding, Salim Karim Iskef managed to video-call his fiancée to tell her he loved her one last time before he died this week in what was Sweden's deadliest mass shooting, The 28-year-old asked Kareen Alia to look after his mother and herself before the call ended.
item.dc:creator: ALEKSANDAR FURTULA, SERGEI GRITS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Frozen US aid brings new challenges to groups hit by harsh crackdowns in Russia and Belarus
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Rights groups, activists and independent media in Russia and Belarus endured increased government scrutiny, repressive laws and even being outlawed and forced to operate from exile abroad. Many survived despite the difficulties.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from placing thousands of USAID workers on leave and giving them 30-day deadline
item.description: A federal judge has dealt President Donald Trump and billionaire ally Elon Musk their first big setback in their dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development, ordering a temporary halt to plans to pull thousands of agency staffers off the job.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Arab, Muslim Americans blast Trump's 'grotesque' Gaza proposal
item.description: Arab American and Muslim leaders are blasting President Trump’s proposal to take over Gaza, warning that it’s antithetical to their beliefs and amounts to ethnic cleansing.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WGN 9

item.title: Future looks dire for UN Palestinian refugee agency, says UNRWA chief
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The head of the U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees said Thursday that while an Israeli ban has not yet forced the agency to cease operations, it faces an “existential threat” in the long run.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy to lead Ukraine’s delegation at Munich Security Conference which JD Vance will attend
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will lead Ukraine’s delegation at the Munich Security Conference next week, which U.S. Vice President J.D.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy wants nukes or NATO; Trump special envoy Kellogg says 'slim and none' chance
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Kyiv should get NATO membership or nukes to defend against Russia as Trump's special envoy heads to Europe for talks with allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI, scientists unite to decipher old scrolls charred by Vesuvius volcano
item.description: Hundreds of papyrus scrolls were found in the 1750s in the remains of a lavish villa at the Roman town of Herculaneum.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Arab nations decry Trump proposal for US to take over Gaza
item.description: Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas called for the United Nations to “protect the Palestinian people.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: When the president was asked about possibly sending in U.S. troops to Gaza, he said America will “do what is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East expert says Israel and the US are back on the same page
item.description: Scholars for Peace in the Middle East Executive Director Asaf Romirowsky spoke with Fox News Digital about how Trump's return to DC is already having an impact on the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: U.S. allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Saudi Arabia says it won't establish ties with Israel without creation of Palestinian state
item.description: Saudi Arabia said on Wednesday it would not establish ties with Israel without the creation of a Palestinian state.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: At least 10 killed at adult education center in what officials say is Sweden's worst mass shooting
item.description: At least 10 people, including the gunman, were killed Tuesday at an adult education center in what Sweden’s prime minister called the country’s worst mass shooting. But a final death toll, a conclusive number of wounded and a motive hadn’t yet been determined hours later.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EU warns Trump it would ‘respond firmly’ to tariffs
item.description: “The EU would respond firmly to any trading partner that unfairly or arbitrarily imposes tariffs on EU goods,” a spokesperson said. “There is a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation
